tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text:
TIL the USSR concealed a bug in a hand-carved Great Seal given as a "gift" in 1945 to the US ambassador that was only discovered by accident in 1951. The bug was designed by Theremin (!) and had no power source or active electronic components.

tweets.name: Andrew Chen
tweets.text:
“ok this startup is cool but …”

1980:
… what if IBM builds this?

1995
… what if Microsoft builds this?

2010
… what if Google builds this?

Today
… what if <huge AI lab> builds this?

reality is, if founders listened to the “what if” pessimists we’d never have any startups

tweets.text: If you’re building a B2B website right now, this is the part everyone rushes and regrets. Tomorrow I’m doing a live session on using AI to turn positioning into a homepage structure and messaging system, plus prompts and templates so you can do it yourself. https://t.co/9j6kpLXvAI

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text:
Ever stare at a homepage and think:

"It’s polished, but it’s not saying anything."

That’s what happens when you start with sections and layouts before you’ve translated positioning into a story.

Tomorrow I’ll walk through the AI workflow I use to build the structure and

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text:
Most AI homepage copy is just expensive filler. Looks good, says nothing.

Tomorrow (Tue Apr 14 @ 10am PT) I’ll show how I use AI the opposite way, to turn positioning into a homepage that has flow, makes sense fast, and leads to action.

Structure, wireframe, messaging system,
